title: vaccinium membranaceum var. rigidum
intro: Vaccinium membranaceum var. rigidum, also known as rigid huckleberry, is a type of shrub native to western North America, belonging to the heath family (Ericaceae). It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly its berries, which contain anthocyanins and other polyphenolic compounds that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of rigid huckleberry include potential anti-cancer properties, cardiovascular protection, and immune system support, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, rigid huckleberry berries are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried or frozen for later use.
